clinical.chief_complaints: Right upper quadrant pain and a right groin swelling that increases on straining, both worsening over past 2 months
clinical.history_of_present_illness: 52-year-old male with two concurrent surgical problems. First, symptomatic cholelithiasis with recurrent biliary colic for the past 3 months, confirmed on ultrasound. Second, a right inguinal hernia noted for the past 6 months, reducible, no prior episodes of obstruction or strangulation. Patient and surgeon have agreed to perform combined laparoscopic surgery: laparoscopic cholecystectomy and laparoscopic mesh repair of right inguinal hernia in the same operative sitting to reduce anaesthesia risk and hospital stay.
clinical.provisional_diagnosis: Symptomatic cholelithiasis with right direct inguinal hernia
clinical.planned_procedure: Combined laparoscopic cholecystectomy (without CBD exploration) and laparoscopic mesh repair of right inguinal hernia (TEP approach)

examination_findings.general: Conscious, well-oriented, no acute distress. No jaundice. No lymphadenopathy.
examination_findings.cvs: S1S2 heard, no murmurs. BP 132/84 mmHg.
examination_findings.abdomen: Mild tenderness in right hypochondrium on deep palpation. Murphy's sign equivocal. A reducible swelling of approximately 3x2cm noted in the right inguinal region, with positive cough impulse. Hernia reduces easily on lying down. Bowel sounds normal.
examination_findings.local: Right inguinal region: reducible, non-tender swelling. Cough impulse positive. Testis normally positioned.

investigations.result_summary: Multiple gallstones in gallbladder (largest 10mm). Gallbladder wall thickness 3.5mm. No pericholecystic fluid. CBD not dilated (3mm). Normal liver parenchyma. Right inguinal hernia confirmed on dynamic USG scan with Valsalva.
WBC 8,400 cells/mcL (normal). Haemoglobin 14.1 g/dL. Total bilirubin 0.8 mg/dL. ALP 74 U/L. AST 28 U/L. ALT 32 U/L. INR 1.0. Serum amylase normal.

clinical.past_medical_history: Hypertension for 5 years, well controlled on amlodipine. No diabetes. No cardiac illness.

non_clinical_documents_in_hand.label: USG Abdomen and Dynamic Inguinal Scan

clinical.notes: Combined laparoscopic approach planned to minimise hospital stay. Pre-anaesthetic fitness obtained. Antihypertensives continued. Polypropylene mesh to be used for hernia repair.
